name: Precision Data — ER Diagram with Exact Types and Cardinalities
input: Create an ER diagram for a biobank database. Entities: DONOR (donor_id PK int, first_name varchar(50), last_name varchar(50), date_of_birth date, blood_type enum(A+,A-,B+,B-,AB+,AB-,O+,O-), consent_date timestamp). SAMPLE (sample_id PK int, donor_id FK int, tissue_type varchar(100), collection_date date, storage_temp decimal(5,2), quantity_ml float, status enum(available,reserved,consumed,destroyed)). STUDY (study_id PK int, pi_name varchar(100), title varchar(255), irb_number varchar(20), start_date date, funding_amount decimal(12,2)). SAMPLE_STUDY (sample_id FK int, study_id FK int, allocation_date date, quantity_used float). Relationships: DONOR ||--o{ SAMPLE (one donor has many samples), SAMPLE }o--o{ STUDY through SAMPLE_STUDY (many-to-many).
qualityCriteria: DONOR entity with donor_id as PK
SAMPLE entity with sample_id PK and donor_id FK
STUDY entity with study_id PK and funding_amount field
DONOR to SAMPLE one-to-many relationship shown
SAMPLE to STUDY many-to-many via junction table
At least 3 attributes per entity with type annotations
Blood type enum values preserved (A+, B+, O-, etc.)
